mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength that support near-term equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a win rate dropping below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4 percent, contradicting the tightening expectation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income growth and consumer expenditure, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sub-1.5 percent quarterly GDP growth and declining retail sales confirms a broad demand shortfall that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: The joint condition of weak GDPC1 and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, yielding a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts signal renewed confidence in the residential construction sector and associated supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during policy easing cycles, with directional accuracy dropping below 45 percent across a 3-year evaluation period.
